title: Test Strategy, Contracts, Properties, and Mutation · Gate Lens: Behavior Contracts
summary: Design a layered test suite around risks rather than file counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates behavior contracts increment in a risk-to-test coverage matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test public behavior, invariants, boundaries, and failure recovery.
Assign unit, integration, contract, browser, system, and acceptance tests to distinct risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Build Behavior Contracts evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-behavior-contracts-pf7pny: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Behavior Contracts into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-behavior-contracts-pf7pny:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Behavior Contracts structural check is only one gate. a risk-to-test coverage matrix must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test public behavior, invariants, boundaries, and failure recovery.
